title: Catch Semantics, Collection Identity, Randomness, and Deterministic Evidence
description: Catch uses a hidden global random source, stores partial response pointers, duplicates the same Pokemon unpredictably, and makes tests depend on luck and request timing. Learners cumulatively produce deterministic owned Pokemon collection transition through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define whether catch is guaranteed, probabilistic, skill-based, or a collection action and state duplicate and failure semantics.
If probability is used, inject a narrow source, record seed or draw evidence, bound values, and keep policy separate from randomness.
Key owned entries by resolved stable ID while retaining display name and capture evidence without raw-input duplicates.
